title: Fix: add accessibility attributes to frontend
close_reason: Added accessibility attributes across all 3 pages + components: (1) Upload page: drop zone has role=button, tabIndex, aria-label, keyboard handler (Enter/Space); hidden input has aria-hidden; text inputs have aria-label; progress bar has role=progressbar with aria-valuenow/min/max; error has role=alert. (2) Feed page: select has aria-label; th elements have scope=col; sortable headers have aria-sort, tabIndex, keyboard handlers. (3) Detail page: processing status has aria-live=polite; failed state has role=alert; category card toggle has aria-expanded. (4) ScoreGauge: wrapper has role=img with aria-label. Build verified clean.

title: MVP Infrastructure as Code (Bicep)
description:
## Overview

Complete Bicep IaC to provision the entire MVP from scratch. One `./infra/deploy.sh` on a fresh subscription stands up everything and deploys code. No manual Azure Portal clicks required.

## Prerequisites

- Azure CLI installed and logged in (`az login`)
- Subscription with Owner role
- Node.js (for func CLI and swa CLI — auto-installed by deploy.sh if missing)

## deploy.sh — One Script to Rule Them All

```
./infra/deploy.sh              # Deploy everything (infra + backend + frontend)
./infra/deploy.sh infra        # Infrastructure only (Bicep + Key Vault secrets)
./infra/deploy.sh code         # Code only (backend + frontend)
./infra/deploy.sh backend      # Function App code only
./infra/deploy.sh frontend     # Static Web App only
./infra/deploy.sh status       # Show what is deployed
./infra/deploy.sh teardown teardown   # Destroy everything (double confirmation)
```

The script auto-detects what needs doing:
- Checks which resource providers are registered, only registers missing ones
- Purges soft-deleted Key Vault and Cognitive Services resources (blocks redeploy with same names)
- Grants deploying user Key Vault Secrets Officer with retry loop for RBAC propagation
- Links SWA backend to Function App (frontend calls /api/* directly, no CORS needed)

## What Bicep Creates (Everything)

### 1. AI Speech Service
- Name: psr-speech-dev, Kind: SpeechServices
- SKU: F0 (free tier — 5 hrs/mo transcription, covers ~7 sermons)
- Location: eastus2
- Fast transcription API proven in POC #6 (3,762 words in 44s)

### 2. Azure OpenAI Account
- Name: psr-openai-dev, Kind: OpenAI
- SKU: S0 (standard)
- Location: eastus2
- No custom subdomain — existing resource uses regional endpoint. Key-based auth via Key Vault secrets works without one. If managed identity auth is needed later, add custom subdomain then.
- Public network access: Enabled

### 3. Azure OpenAI Model Deployments
- `o4-mini`: model o4-mini, version 2025-04-16, Standard, 80K TPM — Pass 1: Biblical Analysis
- `gpt-41`: model gpt-4.1, version 2025-04-14, Standard, 50K TPM — Pass 2: Structure & Content
- `gpt-41-mini`: model gpt-4.1-mini, version 2025-04-14, Standard, 80K TPM — Pass 3: Delivery + Classification

Note: Model versions may need updating if deploying later. Check `az cognitiveservices model list --location eastus2 --query "[?model.name=='o4-mini']"` for current versions.

### 4. Storage Account (Blob)
- Name: psrstoragedev (globally unique)
- SKU: Standard_LRS, Kind: StorageV2
- Container: sermon-audio (private)
- Also serves as AzureWebJobsStorage for the Function App runtime (no second storage account needed)

### 5. Cosmos DB (Serverless)
- Account: psr-cosmos-dev, API: NoSQL
- Capacity: Serverless ($0 at idle)
- Database: psr
- Container: sermons (partition key: /id) — metadata, transcript, PSR scores, status

### 6. Key Vault
- Name: psr-kv-dev, SKU: Standard
- Access: RBAC (no access policies)
- Secrets auto-populated by deploy.sh:
  - speech-key, speech-endpoint
  - openai-key, openai-endpoint, openai-api-version (2025-01-01-preview — required for o4-mini reasoning model)
  - cosmos-connection-string
  - storage-connection-string

### 7. Log Analytics Workspace
- Name: psr-logs-dev, SKU: PerGB2018, Retention: 30 days
- Required dependency for App Insights

### 8. Application Insights
- Name: psr-insights-dev, Kind: web
- Linked to Log Analytics workspace

### 9. Function App (Python, Consumption)
- Name: psr-functions-dev
- Runtime: Python 3.12, OS: Linux, Plan: Consumption (Y1)
- functionTimeout: 00:10:00 (set in host.json, not Bicep — single source of truth)
- App Settings:
  - AzureWebJobsStorage: direct storage connection string (NOT via Key Vault — runtime needs it at cold start before managed identity is ready)
  - APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING: direct (same reason)
  - WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE: 1 (required for consistent Python deployment on Linux consumption)
  - All others via Key Vault references: SPEECH_KEY, SPEECH_ENDPOINT, OPENAI_KEY, OPENAI_ENDPOINT, OPENAI_API_VERSION, COSMOS_CONNECTION_STRING, STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING
- Managed Identity: System-assigned
- CORS: configured for SWA origin + howwas.church + localhost:3000 (fallback if linked backend not used)

### 10. Static Web App
- Name: psr-web-dev, SKU: Free
- Build: Next.js. Bicep creates resource; deploy.sh deploys code via SWA CLI.
- Linked backend: deploy.sh runs `az staticwebapp backends link` after Bicep to proxy /api/* to Function App. This means the frontend just calls `/api/sermons` — no CORS, no separate Function App URL needed.

### 11. RBAC Assignments (Function App managed identity)
- Key Vault Secrets User on psr-kv-dev
- Storage Blob Data Contributor on psrstoragedev
- Cognitive Services User on psr-openai-dev + psr-speech-dev
- Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor on psr-cosmos-dev

## Bicep File Structure

```
infra/
  main.bicep           — orchestrator, calls all modules
  main.bicepparam      — dev environment parameter values
  modules/
    speech.bicep       — AI Speech service (F0)
    openai.bicep       — OpenAI account + 3 model deployments
    storage.bicep      — storage account + blob container
    cosmos.bicep       — Cosmos DB serverless + database + container
    keyvault.bicep     — Key Vault
    monitoring.bicep   — Log Analytics + App Insights
    functions.bicep    — Function App + consumption plan + settings + CORS
    staticwebapp.bicep — Static Web App (bare resource, backend linked by deploy.sh)
    rbac.bicep         — all managed identity role assignments
  deploy.sh            — one script: infra + code + teardown + status
```

## Cost at Idle: ~$0.51/mo

Storage ~$0.50, everything else $0 (serverless/free tiers). Per-sermon: ~$0.75.

title: Fix: add STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING to local.settings.json
close_reason: Added STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING to local.settings.json Values. Was missing, which would break local dev for upload/blob operations. Also verified all other env vars match what function_app.py and activities.py reference.

title: End-to-end integration test — upload through deployed pipeline
description:
Upload a real sermon (Piper "Called According") through the fully deployed pipeline and verify:
1. Upload endpoint accepts file and returns sermon ID
2. Blob storage receives the audio
3. Durable orchestrator runs all passes
4. Cosmos DB document is complete with all 8 scores + transcript + segments
5. GET /api/sermons/{id} returns the full document
6. GET /api/sermons includes it in the feed list
7. Composite score is in the expected range (Piper baseline: ~87-88)

Run after pipeline + API are deployed. Uses the same Piper sermon that all POCs benchmarked against.

title: Configure howwas.church DNS → Azure Static Web App
description:
## Steps

1. Deploy Azure Static Web App (depends on infra Bicep + frontend scaffold)
2. Get the auto-generated hostname (e.g. `xxx.azurestaticapps.net`)
3. In Porkbun DNS, add CNAME: `howwas.church` → `xxx.azurestaticapps.net`
4. In Azure Portal → Static Web App → Custom domains → Add `howwas.church`
5. Azure auto-provisions SSL cert
6. Add `www` redirect if desired

## Blocked By
- Static Web App deployment (sermon-3ch scaffold + sermon-1a3 infra)

title: POC #10: Redefine Time in the Word as biblical content density
close_reason: Redefined Time in the Word from direct quotation % to biblical content density. Spurgeon Compel Them: 85.9→92.7, Immutability: 72.8→87.5. Piper: 83.9→87.7. Scheer correctly drops 84.7→78.7 (30% anecdotes). Updated prompts in all 3 POC files + sermonplan + research docs.

title: Request Azure OpenAI quota for GPT-5-mini, GPT-5-nano, GPT-4.1-nano
description:
## Quota Request

Submit form at https://aka.ms/oai/stuquotarequest

- Subscription: 80b31d19-b663-4936-b8ee-93f7af5b1d27
- Resource: psr-openai-dev
- Region: East US 2

### Models to Request

| Model | Deployment Type | Requested TPM |
|---|---|---|
| gpt-5-mini | GlobalStandard | 50K |
| gpt-5-nano | GlobalStandard | 50K |
| gpt-4.1-nano | GlobalStandard | 50K |

### Current Deployments (working, no changes needed)
- o4-mini (Pass 1: Biblical Analysis) — KEEP, best reasoning model available
- gpt-41 / GPT-4.1 (Pass 2: Structure & Content)
- gpt-41-mini / GPT-4.1-mini (Pass 3: Delivery + Classification)

### Upgrade Path Once Quota Approved
- gpt-5-mini replaces GPT-4.1 for Pass 2 — better benchmarks, 75% cheaper ($0.25/$2.00 vs $2.00/$8.00)
- gpt-5-nano replaces GPT-4.1-mini for Pass 3 + classification — 85% cheaper ($0.05/$0.40 vs $0.40/$1.60)
- gpt-4.1-nano is fallback for classification if GPT-5 quota takes a while

### Cost Impact
Current: ~$0.086/sermon → After upgrade: ~$0.040/sermon (-53%)

### Notes
- GPT-5 quota requests may take days to weeks per Microsoft forums
- o4-mini stays for Pass 1 — purpose-built for reasoning, proven in POCs
- Nothing is blocked; current models are solid

title: API Layer (Azure Functions HTTP triggers)
description:
Three HTTP endpoints the frontend needs, running as Azure Functions:

## POST /api/sermons — Upload

Accept audio upload, validate, kick off processing.

Validation:
- File type: MP3, WAV, M4A only (check Content-Type + magic bytes)
- File size: max 100MB (Azure Functions consumption plan request limit)
- WAV files over 100MB: reject with error suggesting MP3 conversion. Fast transcription API accepts MP3 directly — no need to convert to WAV.
- Duration check: not possible pre-upload without processing. WPM sanity check catches bad files post-transcription.

**Note:** Frontend performs client-side file size and type validation before upload (check file.size and file.type in JS). This prevents users from hitting the Functions 100MB request limit with a confusing server-side error. Backend still validates as a safety net.

Flow:
1. Validate file
2. Create sermon record in Cosmos DB (status: processing, title: user-provided or filename, pastor: user-provided or null, uploadedAt: now)
3. Store audio in Blob Storage (sermon-audio container, key: {id}/{original_filename})
4. Start Durable Functions orchestrator (pass sermon ID + blob URL + user-provided metadata)
5. Return sermon ID immediately

Response: {id, status: processing}

## GET /api/sermons — Feed List

Return all sermons for the feed page. No pagination for MVP.

Response: [{id, title, pastor, date, duration, status, sermonType, compositePsr}]
- status values: processing, complete, failed
- compositePsr is null when status is processing or failed
- title/pastor come from user input (if provided) or LLM metadata extraction (step 4 of pipeline)
- If still processing and no user-provided title, defaults to original filename

## GET /api/sermons/{id} — Full Detail

Return full sermon document from Cosmos DB. Frontend reads: metadata, all 8 category scores + weights + reasoning, composite, strengths/improvements, transcript with segment classifications, radar chart data.

When status is failed:
- error: human-readable error message
- failedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp
- compositePsr, categories, transcript, strengths, improvements: all null

## CORS

Configure for Static Web App origin. In dev: localhost:3000.

## Data Contracts

Defined in docs/frontend-spec.md. The API returns Cosmos DB documents directly — no transformation layer for MVP.

title: Sermon Processing Pipeline (Durable Functions)
description:
Durable Functions orchestrator that takes uploaded audio through the full pipeline:

1. Transcribe via Azure AI Speech fast transcription API
2. Audio analysis via Parselmouth (pitch, volume, pauses)
   (Steps 1 and 2 run in parallel — neither depends on the other)

3. Fan-out 5 parallel passes (all need transcript; Pass 3 also needs Parselmouth data):
   - Pass 1: Biblical Analysis (o4-mini) — Biblical Accuracy, Time in the Word, Passage Focus
   - Pass 2: Structure & Content (GPT-4.1) — Clarity, Application, Engagement
   - Pass 3: Delivery (GPT-4.1-mini) — Delivery, Emotional Range
   - Sermon type classification + metadata extraction (GPT-4.1-mini) — expository/topical/survey, title, pastor name, main passage
   - Segment classification (GPT-4.1-mini) — label transcript segments as scripture/teaching/application/anecdote/illustration/prayer/transition (required for frontend transcript viewer color-coding)

4. Fan-in

5. Score normalization by sermon type (pure code, no LLM) — needs sermon type from classification

6. Store results in Cosmos DB, update status to complete

## Parselmouth Hosting

Parselmouth is a single 36MB .so with standard libc deps (no exotic native binaries). It CAN run on Azure Functions Linux consumption plan — the sandbox supports standard C++ libs. The 36MB adds to cold start but is within the 1.5GB memory limit. For MVP, deploy Parselmouth as part of the Function App package. If cold starts become a problem, move to a dedicated activity function on Premium plan later.

## Parselmouth Noise Floor Filtering (POC #8)

POC #8 (Scheer sermon) reported 384 dB intensity range — physically impossible. Caused by near-silence frames in the MP3. Fix: filter out frames below a noise floor (bottom 5th percentile of intensity values) before computing intensity stats. Pitch already filters unvoiced frames (pv > 0); apply the same pattern to intensity. Expected real range: 30-80 dB.

## Sermon Type Classification Fix (POC #8)

POC #8 misclassified Scheer's verse-by-verse 1 Peter 1:1-2 sermon as topical (85%) because the classifier only saw the first 2000-3000 chars — which was all intro anecdote. This inflated the composite by ~3 points via normalization bumps.

Fix: send more transcript context to the classifier — first 1500 + middle 1500 + last 1500 chars instead of just first 2000-3000. Alternatively, add a post-classification sanity check: if Pass 1 detects a clear main passage with >40% focus AND scripture density >0.5 refs/min, override topical→expository.

## Error/Retry Strategy

Durable Functions built-in retry policies:
- Transcription (fast API): retry 2x, 30s backoff. If permanent fail → status: failed, error: transcription_error
- Parselmouth audio analysis: retry 1x. If fail → proceed WITHOUT audio metrics, flag delivery/emotional_range scores as partial (audio_metrics: null). Graceful degradation.
- Each LLM pass (1-3): retry 3x, exponential backoff starting at 60s (not 10s — POC #8 showed 429s need 60-180s waits on S0 tier). Stagger parallel pass launches by 2-3s to avoid simultaneous token bursts against the shared TPM quota.
- Classification + metadata: retry 2x, 60s backoff. If fail → default to type: unknown, title: filename, pastor: unknown
- Segment classification: retry 1x. If fail → store transcript without segment labels, frontend shows unsegmented text
- Overall orchestrator timeout: 10 minutes
- On any permanent failure: update Cosmos DB status to failed with error message and failed_at timestamp

Note: S0 Standard tier rate limits (50-80K TPM) are tight for longer sermons. An 8K-word transcript (normal for 45-50 min sermons) with 3-4 parallel passes easily hits the ceiling. POC #8 confirmed this is not an edge case. If rate limits become a persistent problem, options: (1) request quota increase, (2) stagger passes sequentially instead of parallel (adds ~30s latency), (3) upgrade to higher tier.

## WPM Sanity Check

After transcription, check WPM. If < 80 or > 200, flag as suspect but continue processing. Store wpm_flag: true in results so frontend can show a warning.

## Upload Retry

No resume mechanism for failed uploads in MVP. If upload fails mid-transfer (network drop), user must retry from scratch. Frontend shows clear error with retry option. Phase 1: consider chunked upload or direct-to-blob via SAS token.

## Depends On

Infra (sermon-1a3) being deployed. POC #7 validates the full pipeline produces accurate scores on complete transcripts. POC #8 validates pipeline works across different preachers and sermon lengths (8K words / 48 min).

## Cost Target

~$0.75 per sermon ($0.67 speech + $0.09 OpenAI). POC #7 confirmed $0.09 OpenAI on 3,762-word transcript. POC #8 confirmed $0.76 on 8,192-word transcript.

title: POC #9: Spurgeon historical benchmark (text-only scoring)
close_reason: Scored 3 Spurgeon sermons: Compel Them to Come In (85.9), Immutability of God (72.8), Power of the Holy Spirit (80.7). Text-only scoring validated. Zero 429 errors with throttle-aware scheduling. Results in poc/spurgeon_benchmark_results.json, documented in docs/research.md.

title: Podcast RSS subscription — auto-score new episodes
description: Allow users to subscribe to a podcast RSS feed URL. System polls for new episodes, downloads audio, and automatically runs the PSR pipeline. Enables passive monitoring of pastors/churches without manual uploads. Discovery: DBC Morning Messages feed (dentonbible.org/mediafiles/denton-bible-morning-messages.xml) has 274+ episodes with direct MP3 enclosure URLs — standard podcast RSS makes this straightforward.

title: POC #7: Validate multipass scoring on full transcript
description: Re-run POC #5 multipass scoring on POC #6's complete transcript (3,762 words vs 1,064). Validates that scores are accurate on full text. Result: composite 88.0 vs 82.7 — partial transcript underestimated by 5.3 points. Application +13, Clarity +10, Delivery +10. Scripture refs: 12 vs 6. See poc/validated_multipass_poc.py and docs/research.md POC #7 section.
close_reason: Completed. Composite PSR 88.0 on full transcript (vs 82.7 on partial). All findings documented in docs/research.md and poc/README.md.

title: Fix: add segment classification batching for long sermons
close_reason: Added batching at 200 segments to classify_segments(). Research: 48-min Scheer sermon produces 644 phrases from fast transcription. Token count (~11K) is within limits, but model accuracy degrades on 600+ item JSON arrays. Batching at 200 keeps each call reliable. Pads short responses with 'teaching' default. Verified: file compiles clean.

title: Build MVP Frontend
description:
Full spec at docs/frontend-spec.md. Three pages:

1. Upload page (/) — drag-and-drop audio file, redirect to detail page
2. Sermon feed (/sermons) — ESPN QBR-style sortable table
3. Sermon detail (/sermons/{id}) — Lighthouse-style scorecard with radar chart

Tech: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + Recharts
Deploy: Azure Static Web Apps (free tier)

Key Design Decisions:
- Lighthouse report pattern for the detail page (big score, category grid, expandable reasoning)
- Opta-style 8-axis radar chart as secondary visual
- Three score colors only: green (70+), yellow (50-69), red (below 50)
- Light theme only for MVP
- No component library — Tailwind utilities only
- Poll for processing status (no websockets)

API Contracts:
- POST /api/sermons (upload)
- GET /api/sermons (feed list)
- GET /api/sermons/{id} (full detail)

See docs/frontend-spec.md for wireframes, data contracts, component inventory, and design tokens.
UI inspiration research in bead sermon-3bz.
close_reason: MVP frontend complete. All 3 pages match frontend-spec.md: upload (drag-drop, validation, progress), feed (sortable table, type filter, score colors), detail (270° gauge, 8-category grid with expandable reasoning, Recharts radar, strengths/improvements, color-coded transcript with scripture bolding and legend). Polling for processing status. Failed state handling. SPA navigation via Next.js router. Build clean at 205kB first load for heaviest page.

title: UI Design Research and Inspiration
description:
## UI Design Research — Inspiration Sources and Patterns

### Status
Research complete. Frontend spec written at docs/frontend-spec.md — supersedes the layout proposals below.

### Key Decision Update
Upload page includes optional title and pastor name fields (not in original research). LLM extraction isn't always reliable, so user-provided values take priority when given.

### Design Philosophy
Minimalist. No AI slop. Let the data speak. Every element earns its place.

---

## Sources Researched
1. ESPN QBR Leaderboard — sortable table pattern for sermon feed
2. Opta Player Radars — 8-axis radar chart for sermon detail
3. Rotten Tomatoes Score Box — composite score hero + summary pattern
4. Google Lighthouse — category gauge grid with expandable reasoning
5. Chronicle.church — sermon card design, text-forward list layout
6. Minimal Podcast App — anti-noise philosophy, typography-first
7. QB Throwing Scorecards — screenshot-able shareable card (Phase 1)
8. Credit Score Displays — circular gauge with factors list

All patterns incorporated into docs/frontend-spec.md.
close_reason: Research complete. All patterns incorporated into docs/frontend-spec.md. Superseded by frontend bead sermon-36d.

title: Scaffold Next.js frontend project
description: npx create-next-app in web/ directory. TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + App Router. No component library. Add Recharts for radar chart. Configure for Azure Static Web Apps deployment. See docs/frontend-spec.md for full spec and design tokens.
close_reason: Scaffolded Next.js frontend in web/. TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + App Router + Recharts. Three pages: upload (/), feed (/sermons), detail (/sermons/[id]). Components: ScoreGauge (270° arc), RadarView (8-axis Recharts), TranscriptViewer (color-coded segments). Hybrid rendering (no output:export) for dynamic routes. SWA config + deploy.sh updated for .next hybrid deploy. Build passes clean.

title: Choose vanity domain name for PSR
description:
## Domain Name Research (RDAP-verified 2026-03-02)

## VERIFIED AVAILABLE — Creative TLDs

| Domain | Why |
|--------|-----|
| **howwas.church** | Everyone asks 'how was church?' — THIS is the answer. Instantly memorable. |
| **psr.church** | PSR brand + .church context. 10 chars. |
| **preach.ing** | Reads as 'preaching'. .ing TLD wordplay. |
| **sunday.review** | Monday morning energy. Clean. |
| **sermon.review** | Literal. Says what it is. |
| **sermon.report** | Report card / Lighthouse vibes. |
| **pulpit.review** | Sports desk meets church. |

## VERIFIED AVAILABLE — .com (Azure API)

psrscore.com, pulpitstats.com, pulpitscore.com, sermonrating.com, roastmysermon.com, grademysermon.com, scoremysermon.com, rateyoursermon.com, pastorscore.com, pastorrating.com, sermonreport.com, preacherscore.com

## VERIFIED TAKEN

sermon.ai, psr.ai, psr.app, sermon.app, after.church, ratemy.church, score.church, mypsr.com, getpsr.com, sermoniq.com, sermonscore.com, psr.org, psr.net, thepsr.com, sermonmetrics.com

## Top Picks

1. **howwas.church** — the killer
2. **psr.church** — clean brand
3. **preach.ing** — most creative
4. **sunday.review** — descriptive
5. **psrscore.com** — safe .com fallback

## Next Steps

1. Pick one (or two — primary + redirect)
2. Register via Squarespace or Cloudflare
3. Point DNS to Azure Static Web App
close_reason: Purchased howwas.church via Squarespace (5/yr). Documented in docs/AZURE_SETUP.md. DNS config tracked separately.

title: Build segment classification prompt (transcript color-coding)
description:
The 5th LLM pass in the pipeline — labels transcript segments as scripture/teaching/application/anecdote/illustration/prayer/transition. Required for frontend transcript viewer color-coding (blue/gray/green/orange left borders).

This is the only pipeline pass that was never POC'd. The other 4 passes (biblical, structure, delivery, classification) all have proven prompts in validated_multipass_poc.py.

Needs to:
1. Take timestamped transcript segments from Azure AI Speech
2. Classify each segment into one of 7 types
3. Return JSON array matching the segment structure in the Cosmos schema
4. Use GPT-4.1-mini (same deployment as Pass 3 + classification)

Can be tested against cached transcripts in poc/ before pipeline is built.
close_reason: Segment classification prompt built in api/activities.py classify_segments(). Uses GPT-4.1-mini, classifies each segment into 7 types (scripture/teaching/application/anecdote/illustration/prayer/transition). Returns JSON array matching frontend-spec segment structure.

title: Define MVP Cosmos DB document schema
description:
MVP Cosmos DB schema for the sermons container (partition key: /id). Single document type for MVP.

## Sermon Document Fields

Core: id (uuid), title, pastor, date, duration (seconds), status (processing|complete|failed), sermonType (expository|topical|survey|unknown), classificationConfidence (float 0-1), compositePsr (float|null), error (string|null), failedAt (ISO 8601|null), summary, audioUrl (blob URL).

Categories object: biblicalAccuracy, timeInTheWord, passageFocus, clarity, engagement, application, delivery, emotionalRange — each with score (0-100), weight (int), reasoning (string). All null when status != complete.

Lists: strengths (string[]), improvements (string[]).

Transcript: fullText, wordCount, wordsPerMinute, wpmFlag (true if <80 or >200), segments[] with start/end (seconds), text, type (scripture|teaching|application|anecdote|illustration|prayer|transition).

AudioMetrics (null if textOnly or Parselmouth fails): meanPitch, pitchRange (Hz), meanIntensity, intensityRange (dB), pauseCount, meanPauseDuration (seconds).

ScriptureReferences[]: reference (e.g. Romans 8:28), verification (aligned|debated|contradicts), context.

Metadata: normalizationApplied (full|half|none), textOnly (bool), createdAt, updatedAt.

Full JSON schema in docs/frontend-spec.md lines 302+. Partition key /id — cross-partition feed queries fine at MVP volume.
close_reason: Schema defined in bead description + docs/frontend-spec.md. Added normalizationApplied and classificationConfidence fields to bridge scoring pipeline and API.

title: Throttle-aware rate limit scheduler for LLM passes
close_reason: Implemented throttle-aware scheduler in azure_multipass_poc.py and validated_multipass_poc.py. Estimates tokens per pass, groups by deployment, runs parallel only when safe. Updated research.md and sermonplan.md with learnings.

title: Refinement: move radar chart above category cards for screenshot-friendly fold
description:
Design principle #5 says the top fold (score + summary + radar) should look good shared on Twitter/Discord. Currently the layout order in SermonDetail.tsx is: score gauge → summary → 8 category cards (2×4 grid) → radar chart. The category cards push the radar well below the fold on most viewports.

Fix: move the RadarView component directly below the summary, before the category breakdown grid. This puts all three hero elements (score + summary + radar) in the top fold as the principle intended. Alternative: make category cards collapsed by default so the fold stays compact.

Ref: web/src/components/SermonDetail.tsx, docs/frontend-spec.md principle #5.

title: Refinement: clarify 'three colors' design principle scope in frontend-spec
description:
Design principle #3 says 'Three colors for scores. Green (70+), yellow (50-69), red (below 50). That's it.' The scoring UI follows this perfectly — ScoreGauge, category cards, and feed table all use exactly these three colors.

However, the TranscriptViewer introduces a separate 4-color palette for segment types: blue (scripture), gray (teaching), green (application), orange (anecdote/illustration). This doesn't violate the principle since these aren't scoring colors, but the wording 'That's it' implies no other colors exist in the app.

Suggested fix: update principle #3 in docs/frontend-spec.md to read: 'Three colors for scores. Green (70+), yellow (50-69), red (below 50). Segment-type colors (transcript viewer) are separate and muted.'

Ref: docs/frontend-spec.md line 13, web/src/components/TranscriptViewer.tsx.

title: Refinement: add design principle for processing and error states
description:
The five design principles in docs/frontend-spec.md cover the 'complete' state well but say nothing about loading/error UX. The implementation in SermonDetail.tsx handles three states — processing (polling with step checklist), failed (error message + retry link), and complete (full report). These states are well-built but weren't guided by any principle.

Suggested addition — Principle #6: 'Processing feels purposeful. Show what's happening (transcribing, analyzing, scoring) not just a spinner. Failed states offer a clear next action.'

This codifies what's already implemented and ensures future contributors maintain the pattern rather than regressing to generic loading spinners.

Ref: docs/frontend-spec.md, web/src/components/SermonDetail.tsx lines 61-83.

title: Bug: activity_update_sermon has no retry policy and no error handling
description:
Two related issues in the final pipeline step:

1. function_app.py line 273: activity_update_sermon is called WITHOUT a retry policy. If Cosmos throttles or has a transient error after the entire pipeline succeeds, the sermon stays stuck at 'processing' forever. The frontend polls indefinitely with no timeout.

2. activities.py line 448: update_sermon() does container.read_item() with no try/except. If the document was deleted or Cosmos is temporarily unavailable, the activity crashes with an unhandled CosmosResourceNotFoundError.

Fix: Add RETRY_LIGHT to the activity_update_sermon call in function_app.py, and wrap the read_item in activities.py with proper exception handling.

Ref: api/function_app.py:273, api/activities.py:448

title: Bug: temp file suffix hardcoded to .mp3 for all audio formats
description:
activities.py lines 69 and 123: both transcribe() and analyze_audio() save downloaded audio to a temp file with hardcoded .mp3 suffix regardless of actual format. WAV and M4A files are saved as .mp3.

The Azure Fast Transcription API may use the file extension to determine codec. A WAV file saved as .mp3 could fail transcription or produce garbled results.

Fix: Extract the actual extension from the blob URL (which contains the original filename) and use it for the temp file suffix.

Ref: api/activities.py:69, api/activities.py:123

title: Bug: detail page polling has no timeout, race condition, and no error handling
description:
Three issues in SermonDetail.tsx polling (lines 23-40):

1. NO TIMEOUT: If status stays 'processing' (e.g. pipeline fails to write update), polling runs forever. User stares at 'Analyzing sermon...' until they give up. Add a max polling duration (~10 min) then show 'taking longer than expected' with a retry option.

2. RACE CONDITION: setTimeout is not cleared on unmount. If the component rapidly mounts/unmounts (e.g. React Strict Mode, navigation), multiple concurrent poll loops can run simultaneously. Store the timeout ID and clearTimeout in the cleanup function.

3. NO FETCH ERROR HANDLING: Line 28 does await fetch() with no try/catch. A network error throws an unhandled promise rejection and breaks the component. The feed page (sermons/page.tsx:20-23) has the same issue — fetch failure is silent.

Ref: web/src/components/SermonDetail.tsx:23-40, web/src/app/sermons/page.tsx:20-23

title: Bug: types.test.ts imports CATEGORY_WEIGHTS which is not exported from types.ts
description:
web/src/__tests__/lib/types.test.ts line 4 imports CATEGORY_WEIGHTS from @/lib/types, but types.ts does not export this constant. The test file cannot run — it fails at import time.

Fix: Add CATEGORY_WEIGHTS export to web/src/lib/types.ts matching the backend's schema.py definition (biblicalAccuracy: 25, timeInTheWord: 20, passageFocus: 10, clarity: 10, engagement: 10, application: 10, delivery: 10, emotionalRange: 5).

Ref: web/src/__tests__/lib/types.test.ts:4, web/src/lib/types.ts, api/schema.py:11-20

title: Bug: deploy.sh doesn't set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL before building frontend
description:
The frontend needs NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL set at build time to know where to send API calls. api.ts defaults to empty string (same-origin relative URLs). SWA Free tier does not support linked backends (requires Standard tier), so the frontend must call the Function App directly.

Deploy.sh currently: (1) deploys infra, (2) deploys backend, (3) builds + deploys frontend. But step 3 never sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL to the Function App URL. The built frontend will use relative /api/* URLs which only work if SWA backend linking succeeds — which it won't on Free tier (deploy.sh line 188 swallows the error with || true).

Fix: After deploying the Function App, extract its URL (https://psr-functions-dev.azurewebsites.net) and set NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL before running npm run build. Also need CORS on the Function App for the SWA origin.

Note: The CORS config in functions.bicep (lines 33-37) does include the SWA hostname, but also includes http://localhost:3000 which should not ship to production.

Ref: infra/deploy.sh, web/src/lib/api.ts:2, infra/modules/functions.bicep:33-37

title: Frontend types missing backend fields: wpmFlag, audioMetrics, classificationConfidence, normalizationApplied
description:
The backend returns several fields in GET /api/sermons/{id} that the frontend SermonDetail interface doesn't include: wpmFlag (boolean), audioMetrics (object|null), classificationConfidence (number|null), normalizationApplied (string|null), failedAt (string|null), blobUrl (string), filename (string).

These are silently dropped by TypeScript. Two are user-facing:
- wpmFlag: If true, the sermon has suspect WPM (<80 or >200) and should show a warning badge.
- normalizationApplied: If 'full' or 'half', the score was adjusted for sermon type. Users may want to know why similar sermons scored differently.

Fix: Add missing fields to SermonDetail interface in types.ts. Display wpmFlag as a warning on the detail page. Optionally display normalization info in the category breakdown.

Ref: web/src/lib/types.ts:27-37, api/schema.py:42-67, docs/frontend-spec.md

title: No rate limiting on upload endpoint — concurrent uploads can exhaust LLM quota
description:
POST /api/sermons has no rate limiting. A user (or bot) could upload 100 sermons simultaneously, each kicking off 5+ parallel LLM passes. With S0 Standard tier at 50-80K TPM, even 3-4 concurrent sermons could trigger cascading 429 errors.

The Durable Functions retry policy (RETRY_LLM: 4 attempts, 60s backoff) helps, but multiple orchestrators all retrying at once makes the situation worse.

For MVP: Add a simple concurrency check — query Cosmos for sermons with status='processing', reject new uploads if count exceeds a threshold (e.g., 3). Phase 1: proper queue-based throttling.

Ref: api/function_app.py:39-90, api/host.json (no concurrency config)

title: All POC test data is from elite preachers — need a mediocre sermon benchmark
description:
Every POC used sermons from Spurgeon, Piper, or Scheer — all elite-level preachers scoring 72-88 PSR. The scoring rubric has never been tested against a typical congregation sermon that should score in the 40-65 range.

Risks: (1) Score calibration may be biased toward the high end — a mediocre sermon might still score 70+. (2) The normalization adjustments (topical +5/+8/+10) were calibrated against elite sermons and may over-correct for average ones. (3) Strengths/improvements generation was only tested with high-scoring inputs.

Fix: Find or record a deliberately average sermon. Run it through the pipeline and verify the composite lands in the expected 40-65 range. Adjust rubric anchoring if needed.

Ref: poc/ (all files use Piper/Spurgeon/Scheer), api/schema.py:34-39 (normalization)

title: Verify Parselmouth builds on Azure Functions Linux Consumption (Python 3.12)
description:
Parselmouth (praat-parselmouth) is a 36MB C++ native extension. The sermonplan acknowledges it CAN run on Azure Functions Linux but this has never been tested in the actual deployment. If it fails to load at runtime, the activity_analyze_audio step silently degrades (audio_metrics=None) and delivery/emotional_range scores become text-only estimates.

The graceful degradation path works (function_app.py:178-182), but the scores will be less accurate for every sermon until this is fixed.

Test: Deploy to Azure Functions and call the analyze_audio activity directly. Check Application Insights for any import errors on parselmouth.

Ref: api/requirements.txt:4, api/activities.py:112-158, infra/modules/functions.bicep:30 (Python 3.12)

title: deploy.sh swallows errors and lacks post-deploy validation
description:
Several deploy.sh issues that could leave the system in a broken state:

1. Line 188-191: SWA backend link failure swallowed with || true. If it fails, /api/* calls 404 with no indication.
2. Line 217: func azure functionapp publish output piped through tail -5, hiding errors. If the function app fails to start (e.g. missing Parselmouth), deploy reports success.
3. Lines 174-181: Seven az keyvault secret set calls with no individual error checking. If the 5th one fails, subsequent activity functions crash with missing env vars.
4. No post-deploy smoke test to verify the function app responds to GET /api/sermons.

Fix: Add validation after each critical step. At minimum, after code deploy, curl the health endpoint and verify 200.

Ref: infra/deploy.sh:188, 217, 174-181
